title: Up to $160,000 EdTech Research Grants for Foundational Learning in Kenya, Malawi, and Senegal (Apply by 2 March 2026)
summary: Education research organisations in Kenya, Malawi, and Senegal now have an opportunity to secure up to $160,000 in funding to strengthen evidence-informed EdTech policy and improve foundational learning outcomes. The call is issued under the African EdTech Initiative (AEI), a three-year research programme funded by the International Development Research Centre (IDRC) and implemented by Education Sub Saharan Africa (ESSA), EdTech Hub , and Association for the Development of Education in Africa (ADEA). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This competitive funding aims to empower local research organisations to collaborate directly with Ministries of Education (MoEs) and shape national EdTech strategies that improve foundational learning for all learners. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Application Deadline Deadline: Monday, 2 March 2026, 9:00 AM GMT Late submissions will not be considered. About the African EdTech Initiative (AEI) The African EdTech Initiative seeks to answer a critical policy question: How can governments in sub-Saharan Africa coordinate EdTech research to enable strategic, effective, and cost-efficient improvements in foundational learning? ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The programme works closely with Ministries of Education and local research organisations to generate context-specific, policy-relevant evidence that strengthens national EdTech systems. Through this grant, selected organisations will co-lead country-level action research in partnership with their national Ministry of Education. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Grant Overview Grant Amount: Up to $160,000 Duration: Approximately 18 months Eligible Countries: Kenya, Malawi, Senegal Number of Grants: One per country Each grant will fund: Research design and implementation Coordination with the Ministry of Education Policy engagement activities Dissemination of findings ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The goal is not only to produce research but to ensure findings are integrated into national policy processes and EdTech implementation plans focused on foundational learning. What This Funding Supports Selected research organisations will: Identify priority EdTech research questions with Ministries Co-design and implement country-level EdTech studies Strengthen government capacity to coordinate research agendas Support evidence-informed policymaking Promote sustainable improvements in foundational learning ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); This initiative prioritises local ownership, context-driven solutions, and strong government partnerships. Eligibility Criteria To qualify, applicants must: Be a legally registered entity (university, NGO, research institute, think tank, or private research firm) Be operational in Kenya, Malawi, or Senegal Demonstrate experience in EdTech research with a focus on foundational learning Show capacity for robust, ethical, and policy-relevant research Demonstrate experience working with Ministries of Education or relevant public sector stakeholders Show commitment to gender inclusivity and diversity ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Applicants must also demonstrate understanding of national education systems, curricula, and policymaking processes related to EdTech and foundational learning. Why This Grant Matters Strengthens local research leadership Builds Ministry capacity to coordinate EdTech research Influences national education policy Promotes evidence-based innovation in foundational learning Contributes to long-term improvements in education systems For research organisations committed to improving learning outcomes through technology, this is a high-impact funding opportunity. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); How to Apply Interested organisations should: Review the Call for Proposals Read the Frequently Asked Questions Complete the official Application Form Applications must be submitted before 2 March 2026 at 9:00 AM GMT . For full details and application access Click Here For more grant opportunities check Here The post Up to $160,000 EdTech Research Grants for Foundational Learning in Kenya, Malawi, and Senegal (Apply by 2 March 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Paid Peace and Security Roles in Geneva and Remote (Apply by 2 March 2026)
summary: The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) is inviting applications for two Graduate Professional (Conventional Arms and Ammunition) positions for 2026. This opportunity is ideal for graduate students and recent graduates passionate about international peace, security, and disarmament. Based within the United Nations system and headquartered at the Palais des Nations in Geneva, UNIDIR conducts independent research on disarmament and global security challenges. These six-month positions offer hands-on experience supporting high-level research and policy work related to conventional arms control. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Application Deadline The deadline to apply is midnight Geneva time on 2 March 2026 . Stipend and Financial Information Graduate Professionals will receive: USD 1,331 per month (in-person, Geneva-based) USD 665.50 per month (remote) The stipend is intended as a contribution toward living expenses. Travel, visas, health insurance, accommodation, and related costs are the responsibility of the Graduate Professional or their sponsoring institution. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); About the Conventional Arms and Ammunition Programme UNIDIR s programme works to prevent and mitigate the uncontrolled proliferation and misuse of conventional arms and ammunition. It contributes to sustainable peace and security through: Research on weapons and ammunition management Prevention of armed conflict and armed violence Multi-stakeholder dialogues Advisory support and capacity-building ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The Graduate Professionals will support either: Research Pillar 2: Preventing Armed Conflict and Armed Violence (Remote) Exploratory Research Cell (Geneva-based) Key Responsibilities Selected candidates will: Conduct background research and analysis Support planning and implementation of meetings, workshops, and conferences Assist in administrative coordination and monitoring Draft concise research briefs and documents ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); This role provides exposure to international policy research, UN processes, and global security governance. Eligibility Criteria Education Applicants must: Have graduated within the past year with a university degree (Bachelor s or Master s equivalent), OR Be currently enrolled in a graduate programme ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The role is intended to be full-time. Preferred academic fields include: Politics International Affairs Peace and Conflict Studies Security Studies International Law Experience Professional work experience is not required Interest or experience in conventional arms control is an asset Research, analytical, and writing skills are essential Language Requirements Fluency in English (oral and written) is required Knowledge of French or Spanish is desirable How to Apply Applicants must submit the following as one single PDF document titled: SURNAME_Name_PHP-CL Include: Cover letter Inspira Personal History Form (PHP) Responses (maximum 30 words each) to screening questions ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Send applications to: unidir.recruitment@un.org Subject line must state either: UNIDIR Conventional Arms and Ammunition GPP Remote OR UNIDIR Conventional Arms and Ammunition GPP Geneva-based Incomplete, late, or AI-generated applications will not be processed. Why Apply? Gain direct experience within the United Nations system Contribute to global disarmament and peacebuilding research Work alongside international experts in Geneva or remotely Strengthen your profile for careers in international security For aspiring professionals in peace and security, this is a highly valuable early-career opportunity within a respected UN research institution. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); For more information, visit the official UNIDIR website: Click Here Check for more job opportunities here The post UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Paid Peace and Security Roles in Geneva and Remote (Apply by 2 March 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: International Excellence Scholarships at University of Greater Manchester

Global Excellence Package Available to self-funded applicants from selected countries (including Ghana, Kenya, South Africa, UAE, China, USA, Canada, Malaysia, etc.): Gold Award 4,000 tuition discount (First class or 70% GPA or equivalent) Silver Award 3,000 tuition discount (Upper second or 60% GPA) Bronze Award 2,000 tuition discount ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Eligible for Undergraduate, Postgraduate Taught PhD programmes. 2. International Merit Package For students from all other countries or via an agent: Gold 3,000 Silver Up to 2,000 Bronze Up to 1,000 Applies to Undergraduate, Postgraduate and PhD study. title: Oxford Blavatnik School of Government Pre-Doctoral Research Assistant 2026
summary: If you re a high-achieving graduate or early-career researcher with a passion for economics and public policy, the Pre-Doctoral Research Assistant (PDRA) position at the University of Oxford s Blavatnik School of Government (BSG) is one of the most prestigious stepping stones to a research, policy, or academic career. Role Overview: What You ll Do As a Pre-Doctoral Research Assistant in Economics and Public Policy, you will: Support high-impact research projects in public policy and economics. Work with large administrative datasets (e.g., tax, innovation, patents). Help design research projects , analyze data, and contribute to academic outputs. Gain unique exposure to cutting-edge social science and policy research methods. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This position provides a rare opportunity to build strong research capabilities ahead of doctoral studies or professional research roles in policy institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Eligibility Qualifications To qualify for this competitive role, candidates should meet the following core requirements: 1. Academic Background Bachelor s degree (or equivalent) in Economics, Public Policy, Finance, or a related quantitative discipline. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); 2. Skill Requirements Strong quantitative and analytical skills. Evidence of working with data or research tools (e.g., statistical software). Demonstrated interest in public policy research or economic analysis. 3. Professional Attributes Excellent communication skills. Ability to work independently and collaboratively. Eagerness to contribute to high-quality international research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Benefits of the PDRA Role This role is more than just a job it s a career accelerator: 1. Professional Academic Growth Hands-on experience with policy-relevant economic research. Exposure to Oxford s leading faculty and research culture. Significant advantage for pursuing a DPhil/PhD or policy leadership roles. 2. Work Benefits According to the official job posting: Excellent contributory pension scheme. 38 days annual leave per year. Family-friendly leave and childcare services. Cycle loan scheme and discounted travel options. Opportunities for hybrid working and access to university resources. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); These benefits make this role particularly attractive for early-career researchers seeking work-life balance alongside professional development. Where You ll Be Based Blavatnik School of Government part of the University of Oxford Located in Oxford, UK a globally renowned center of research excellence. Outstanding facilities, academic network, and vibrant intellectual environment. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Students and staff enjoy access to libraries, lecture series, and international policy networks. How to Apply Step-by-Step Prepare Your CV Supporting Statement Explain your academic excellence, research experience, and how you meet the job criteria. Gather References Two academic or professional referees should be ready to support your application. Submit Online Before Deadline Use the official Oxford jobs portal here Complete Interview Stage Shortlisted candidates will be interviewed in late March 2026. title: Apply Now: Erasmus Mundus Visiting Scholarships for Academics and Policy Professionals 2026-2027 — Fully Funded, Prestigious European Opportunity with Competitive Stipends

title: Apply for the SOAS Teaching Scholarships 2026
summary: Are you planning to pursue a full time doctoral degree and gain teaching experience while securing full financial support? The SOAS Teaching Scholarships 2026 offer an exceptional opportunity for doctoral researchers from Africa, Asia, the Middle East, and Black British applicants to access fully funded MPhil/PhD study at SOAS University of London . These scholarships cover full tuition fees and include a maintenance stipend, helping you focus on your research and teaching development without financial worry. This award is part of SOAS s commitment to addressing inequalities in doctoral participation and supporting underrepresented students who are ready to combine academic excellence with impact driven research and teaching. It provides doctoral candidates with financial stability, academic community integration, and valuable teaching experience that strengthens both academic and professional development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); What the Scholarship Offers The SOAS Teaching Scholarships provide extensive financial support to successful applicants who begin a full time MPhil/PhD programme in the 2026/27 academic year . Each scholarship: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Covers full tuition fees for the duration of your three year full time doctoral degree. Includes a maintenance stipend to support living costs, with a minimum amount of approximately 20,622 for the 2026/27 academic year (final figures confirmed closer to September 2026). Offers the opportunity to gain structured teaching experience during your doctoral programme. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); This combination of financial and professional support enables scholars to deepen their research while developing essential teaching skills that are valuable in academia and beyond. Why This Opportunity Matters Doctoral study can be financially and personally demanding, especially for students from underserved regions or backgrounds. The SOAS Teaching Scholarships help remove financial obstacles by providing full tuition coverage and a meaningful stipend, allowing scholars to engage fully in their research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Additionally, gaining structured teaching experience during your PhD enhances your academic profile and prepares you for future roles in higher education, research, and professional sectors. This blend of research and teaching skill development positions you to make significant contributions to your field and community. By applying for the SOAS Teaching Scholarships 2026, you are taking a major step toward achieving your doctoral goals with financial peace of mind and a strong foundation for future research and teaching excellence. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Who Is Eligible The SOAS Teaching Scholarships are open to applicants from a wide range of backgrounds, including: Black British students with permanent UK residency and from specified Black or Black British ethnic groups. Students ordinarily resident in Africa, Asia, or the Middle East . One scholarship is specifically reserved for a Palestinian national who meets the general eligibility criteria, including residence in historic Palestine (Gaza Strip, West Bank, East Jerusalem) or status as a Palestinian citizen in Israel. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); To qualify, applicants must also: Have received or hold an offer of admission to a full time MPhil/PhD research programme at SOAS by the time the scholarship selection panel meets. Meet any English language requirements tied to their programme offer by 10 May 2026 if applicable. Understand that the Teaching Scholarship cannot be held simultaneously with other major scholarships or studentships . This inclusive eligibility structure supports talented scholars from underrepresented regions and backgrounds, helping to build a diverse academic community at SOAS. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); What the Selection Committee Looks For Scholarship applications are reviewed by an advisory panel trained in diversity and inclusive assessment practices. The panel evaluates applications based on: Academic excellence and research potential , demonstrated by previous results, references, and the strength of the research proposal. The widening participation statement , where applicants explain how they meet the eligibility criteria and how the scholarship will benefit their academic journey and future contributions. Strength of academic references and evidence of scholarly promise. The widening participation statement (up to 800 words) is a key part of the application and is an opportunity for applicants to share personal experiences, including overcoming educational or socioeconomic obstacles, being a first generation university student, or having faced other challenges that shaped their academic aspirations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); How to Apply The application process for the SOAS Teaching Scholarships requires you first to apply for admission into a full time MPhil/PhD programme at SOAS. Once you have started your programme application, you can submit your scholarship application through the university s admissions portal. Application Deadline: 21 March 2026, by 12 noon (UK local time) all applications must be received by this time to be considered for the award. Complete applications are essential. Make sure to submit both your doctoral programme application and the teaching scholarship application before the deadline. Late or incomplete submissions will not be considered. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); If no suitable candidates are nominated by the panel, the scholarship may reopen for applications in May 2026 ; check the official SOAS page for more information and apply. Looking for more Opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website! The post Apply for the SOAS Teaching Scholarships 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the SWE India Scholars 2026 – Scholarships for Women in Engineering
summary: The Society of Women Engineers (SWE) India Scholars Program 2026 is a transformative opportunity for women pursuing undergraduate or graduate degrees in engineering, engineering technology, computing, and related technical fields in India. Designed to empower female students at select institutions across the country, these scholarships aim to reduce financial barriers while encouraging women to lead in sectors where they are historically underrepresented. As part of SWE s longstanding mission to promote gender diversity in STEM, the India Scholars Program provides financial assistance to support tuition, fees, and other education-related expenses. By removing financial obstacles, SWE ensures women engineers can focus on their studies, leadership development, and contributions to the engineering community without the added stress of economic constraints. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Why This Opportunity Matters Women continue to be underrepresented in engineering and technical fields worldwide, including India. Financial challenges such as tuition, materials, and living costs can disproportionately impact women pursuing STEM education. The SWE India Scholars Program helps bridge this gap by providing meaningful financial assistance, allowing recipients to focus on learning, innovation, and leadership development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Beyond financial support, SWE scholarships connect students to a global network of women engineers, mentors, and peers. This community offers guidance, professional networking opportunities, and inspiration to pursue leadership roles in engineering and related disciplines. Participating in the India Scholars Program helps students develop the confidence and skills necessary to thrive in competitive STEM careers while fostering a supportive community of like-minded women. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Who Can Apply The SWE India Scholars Program is open to students who meet the following criteria: Identify as women, including transgender women and individuals who identify as women. Are enrolled full-time in an undergraduate or graduate program in engineering, engineering technology, computing, or a related technical discipline. Attend a program accredited by India s National Board of Accreditation (NBA) or a university that maintains an active SWE Global Affiliate partnership. Can demonstrate academic commitment and leadership potential within their institution and community. Importantly, SWE membership is not required to apply, making the scholarship accessible to all eligible students across India. This inclusive approach ensures that talented women from diverse backgrounds can access financial support for their engineering education. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); What the Scholarship Covers SWE India Scholars awards provide financial support that can be used toward: Tuition and mandatory fees for coursework. Living expenses related to academic study. Educational materials, including textbooks, lab supplies, and project costs. The scholarship amounts vary depending on the program and available funding, with awards typically ranging from $1,000 to $20,000 per recipient . Many scholarships are renewable, offering multi-year support for students who maintain academic excellence and demonstrate ongoing leadership in their programs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Application Process The SWE India Scholars 2026 application is now open for the 2026-27 academic year . Applicants should complete the online application through the SWE website and submit all required documentation, including transcripts and reference details. Once submitted, the single application system automatically considers applicants for all scholarships for which they are eligible, simplifying the process for busy students. The review process evaluates academic achievement, leadership potential, and the candidate s commitment to advancing women in engineering and technical fields. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); How to Get Started To begin your application or learn more about eligibility requirements, visit the official SWE scholarships page: your documents, highlight your leadership experience, and submit your application before March 31, 2026 , to take advantage of this exciting opportunity. The SWE India Scholars Program not only provides financial support but also invests in the next generation of women engineers who will shape the future of technology, innovation, and leadership in India. By applying, students step into a community committed to empowering women, expanding access to STEM education, and fostering professional growth in engineering and technical disciplines. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Looking for more Opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website. The post Apply for the SWE India Scholars 2026 – Scholarships for Women in Engineering appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for Cohort 4 and Grow Your Education Technology Startup in Africa
summary: African innovators developing technology to transform education now have a powerful opportunity to scale, refine, and grow their solutions. The Future of Learning EdTech Fellowship Cohort 4 is a competitive accelerator for early-stage education technology startups building products that enhance learning outcomes, especially for underserved learners across the continent. Supported by iHUB in partnership with the Mastercard Foundation , this Fellowship offers funding, mentorship, technical support, and connections to global investors to help transform and expand impactful EdTech ventures. The goal of the Fellowship is to strengthen the future of learning by empowering EdTech startups that address real educational challenges from inclusive tools for learners with disabilities to tech that supports teachers, caregivers, and school systems. By participating in the program, innovators get access to resources that help refine product design, validate market fit, and accelerate growth. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); What the EdTech Fellowship Offers Participants accepted into Cohort 4 of the Future of Learning EdTech Fellowship receive a suite of support designed to accelerate the development and impact of their education technology solutions: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Equity-free funding Selected startups will receive up to $100,000 in non-equity funding to support product development, scaling strategies, and operational growth throughout the program period. Mentorship and technical support Startups gain direct access to expert mentors across technology, product design, learning science, business strategy, and marketing. Connections to investors and ecosystem partners Through iHUB s network and Mastercard Foundation connections, participating ventures build relationships that help them attract follow-on capital and market opportunities. Peer community and learning Fellowship members join a cohort of like-minded EdTech innovators, creating opportunities for collaboration, knowledge exchange, and shared growth. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Startups in previous cohorts credit the program s mentorship, strategic support, and network access with enhancing their product offerings, strengthening go-to-market strategies, and even securing new partnerships or funding. Startups have also used the guidance to refine their educational content, broaden user adoption, and boost impact across Kenya and the wider African learning ecosystem. Why This Fellowship Matters Education technology is transforming how learners access information, engage with content, and participate in modern learning environments. In many parts of Africa, EdTech innovations are closing gaps left by traditional systems especially for learners with limited resources, those in remote or conflict-affected areas, and those with disabilities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); However, building a sustainable EdTech company requires more than a strong product idea. Ventures need strategic guidance, business development support, investment readiness, and traction-oriented mentoring all of which the Future of Learning EdTech Fellowship provides. By equipping startups with tools and networks, the program helps ensure that promising solutions can grow beyond pilot stages and build lasting impact. In past cohorts, ventures have achieved significant milestones. For example, an earlier cohort of the Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship supported startups that reached hundreds of thousands of learners, strengthened institutional adoption, and expanded access to personalized digital schooling across Kenya. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The emphasis on inclusive design, learning science, and real-world scalability ensures that Kenya s and Africa s education systems benefit from innovations that are both locally relevant and globally competitive. Who Should Apply The Fellowship is open to early-stage EdTech startups that meet the following criteria: Africa-focused solutions Products should be designed to address educational challenges across African contexts, including K-12 learning, post-secondary education, vocational training, or out-of-school learning. Incorporated in Kenya Startups must be formally registered in Kenya to qualify. User-focused impact The solution must demonstrate alignment with improving learner access, teacher effectiveness, school management, or other education outcomes. Inclusive teams encouraged Teams with gender balance and diverse perspectives particularly female founders are strongly encouraged to apply. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); This Fellowship is ideal for founders who are ready to transition from early proof of concept to scaling solutions that reach broader markets and deeper impact. Application Timeline and How to Apply Applications for Cohort 4 of the Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship opened in February 2026. At the end of the application period, a selection process will identify up to twelve EdTech startups to join the Fellows for the 12-month accelerator program. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Applications close: 10 March 2026 Interested founders can submit their applications and learn more at the official Future of Learning website . Looking for more opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website! The post Apply for Cohort 4 and Grow Your Education Technology Startup in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Call for Mentors in Conservation: Empower the Next Generation of Women Leaders with Wildlife Conservation Action
summary: Across Africa and beyond, conservation efforts are becoming increasingly complex, community-driven, and urgently needed. Climate change, biodiversity loss, and growing pressure on natural resources require strong, informed leadership that can navigate both ecological and social challenges. Yet despite their experience and contributions, women remain underrepresented in leadership roles across the conservation sector. The Call for Mentors in Conservation by Wildlife Conservation Action (WCA) seeks to address this gap by inviting established women in conservation to step forward and guide the next generation of female leaders. This mentorship opportunity is designed specifically for mid- to late-career women professionals in conservation who are ready to share their experience, wisdom, and leadership insights. By becoming a mentor, participants play a vital role in strengthening women s leadership pipelines, fostering sisterhood across the sector, and shaping a more inclusive and resilient future for conservation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Why Mentorship in Conservation Matters Mentorship is one of the most powerful tools for professional growth, yet access to mentors remains uneven, particularly for women working in conservation and environmental fields. Careers in conservation are often shaped by field realities, long hours, and complex community dynamics, making informal knowledge and lived experience just as valuable as formal training. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); For early-career women, guidance from experienced professionals can be transformative. Mentors help navigate career decisions, leadership challenges, and moments of uncertainty while offering reassurance, perspective, and encouragement. At the same time, mentors themselves benefit by reflecting on their journeys, strengthening professional networks, and contributing to lasting change within the sector. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); By calling on experienced women to mentor others, Wildlife Conservation Action recognizes that building strong conservation outcomes depends not only on technical solutions, but also on investing in people and leadership across generations. About Wildlife Conservation Action Wildlife Conservation Action is a Zimbabwe-based conservation organization working at the intersection of biodiversity protection, community empowerment, and human-wildlife coexistence . The organization focuses on landscapes where people and wildlife share space, particularly in regions experiencing high levels of human-wildlife conflict. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Through locally grounded approaches, WCA supports both ecological sustainability and community wellbeing. Its work emphasizes collaboration with local communities, recognizing that conservation solutions are most effective when they are inclusive, culturally informed, and locally led. WCA s priority action areas include: Human-wildlife conflict mitigation Environmental education and awareness Climate-smart livelihoods Applied research and monitoring Conservation leadership ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); These focus areas reflect the organization s belief that long-term conservation success relies on strong leadership, shared knowledge, and community participation. The Call for Mentors in Conservation builds directly on this philosophy by placing leadership development and mentorship at the center of conservation practice. Who This Mentorship Opportunity Is For This call is aimed at established women in conservation , particularly those who are mid- to late-career and ready to give back to the sector. Mentors may come from a wide range of backgrounds, including wildlife conservation, environmental management, research, community-based conservation, policy, education, or advocacy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); What unites mentors is not a specific job title, but a willingness to: Share professional experiences, lessons learned, and leadership insights Support early-career women navigating conservation pathways Strengthen networks and solidarity among women in conservation Help create spaces where women can lead confidently and authentically Mentors are invited to lead with purpose, generosity, and openness, recognizing the value of both successes and challenges in shaping future leaders. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The Impact of Becoming a Mentor By participating in this mentorship initiative, women in conservation contribute to more than individual career development. They help: Retain talented women within the conservation sector Build confidence and leadership capacity among emerging professionals Transfer knowledge that is rarely captured in formal training Challenge gender imbalances in conservation leadership Mentorship strengthens the sector as a whole by ensuring that experience, skills, and institutional knowledge are not lost, but passed on to those who will carry conservation work forward. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); How to Apply Women interested in becoming mentors are invited to apply through the official online form. The form allows applicants to share their background, experience, and motivation for joining the mentorship initiative. Apply to become a mentor with this google form . For more information about Wildlife Conservation Action s work and conservation focus areas, visit their website . Looking for more opportunities? Visit the Opportunities for Youth website to explore additional leadership, mentorship, and professional development programs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); The post Call for Mentors in Conservation: Empower the Next Generation of Women Leaders with Wildlife Conservation Action appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
